rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective copper-catalyzed intramolecular alkene carboamination and carboetherification of alkenylsulfonamides/alcohols using O2 (air or 10% O2) as the sole stoichiometric oxidant

key_intermediates.name: Cu(II)-bis(oxazoline) complex coordinated to alkene/sulfonamide substrate
primary carbon radical formed after intramolecular C-X bond formation (X = O, NSO2Ar) onto alkene
cyclized C-radical adding intramolecularly onto aryl ring of N-tosyl/aryl group
Cu(I) species reoxidized by O2 to Cu(II)

electron_flow: Cu(II)·L coordinates the tethered heteroatom nucleophile and alkene; intramolecular C-X bond formation generates a primary carbon radical with concomitant reduction to Cu(I); the C-radical adds onto the pendant arene and rearomatizes to give the carbocyclized product, while Cu(I) is reoxidized to Cu(II) by O2 to close the catalytic cycle.

probe_reagents.name: K2CO3 additive control
probe_reagents.observation: Little conversion (entry 8); proposed to sequester protons required for O2 -> H2O reduction, impeding Cu reoxidation

probe_reagents.name: O2 partial-pressure variation (1 atm O2 vs air vs 10% O2)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower O2 (air/10%) suppresses competitive intermolecular trapping of C-radical by O2 (aldehyde 4, lactam 5) and improves sultam 2 yield

probe_reagents.name: Anaerobic control (no O2, entry 7)
probe_reagents.observation: Mostly starting material with traces of sultam 2 and aminochloride 3; confirms O2 is required for catalytic turnover (Cu(I)->Cu(II) reoxidation)

probe_reagents.name: Achiral vs chiral bis(oxazoline) ligand screen (L1 vs L2)
probe_reagents.observation: CuCl·L1 gives racemic sultam in good yield under air/10% O2; Cu(OTf)2·L2 under O2 gives poor reactivity and ee with competing aminooxygenation/aminochlorination

notes: Mechanism is inferred by analogy to the authors' prior MnO2-oxidant carboamination/carboetherification (ref 13) and TEMPO/O2 aminooxygenation (ref 10/11a) systems shown in Scheme 1A/B. The paper focuses on demonstrating O2 (air) as terminal oxidant in place of MnO2 or TEMPO/O2; detailed mechanistic probes (TEMPO trapping, radical clocks, KIE, DFT) are not performed on this page. Side pathways (aminooxygenation to aldehyde 4, gamma-lactam 5, aminochlorination 3) are diagnostic of a discrete C-radical intermediate. Counterion (OTf vs Cl/carboxylate) and oxidant identity strongly influence whether enantioselective carboamination or racemic/competing pathways dominate.
